seoTitle: Hansborough vs Happy Valley — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Hansborough and Happy Valley. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Hansborough vs Happy Valley: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Hansborough and Happy Valley.
heading: Hansborough vs Happy Valley — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Hansborough, South Australia and Happy Valley, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Happy Valley has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Hansborough (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Hansborough or Happy Valley safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Hansborough has a flood risk score of 58/100, while Happy Valley scores 22/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Hansborough scores 49/100 for bushfire risk. Happy Valley scores 49/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Hansborough or Happy Valley?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Happy Valley has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.55% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
